titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Afro-American Cultural Center at Yale Records
Afro-American Cultural Center at Yale Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 25, 2020 at 07:19
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Afro-American Cultural Center at Yale records
did.abstract: The records consist of correspondence, reports, minutes and agendas, programs, newsletters, news clippings, photographs and digital images, oral histories, exhibit records, and posters documenting the activities and programs of the Afro-American Cultural Center and African-Americans at Yale.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 37 (YRG 37), Records of associations, institutes, centers, and organizations affiliated with Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection requires permission of the Director of the Afro-American Cultural Center. Additional restrictions may apply in certain accessions. See Collection Contents for details.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials may not be played. Researches must consult use copies. If a use copy does not exist for a particular item researchers should consult with a reference archivist for policies and procedures governing the creation of duplicating masters and use copies.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Afro-American Cultural Center at Yale Records (RU 957). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Afro-American Cultural Center at Yale (originally called Afro-America Cultural Center) was established in 1968 in response to concerns raised by the Black Student Alliance at Yale [BSAY).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of correspondence, reports, minutes and agendas, programs, newsletters, news clippings, photographs and digital images, oral histories, exhibit records, and posters documenting the activities and programs of the Afro-American Cultural Center and African-Americans at Yale.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 3,4,7,14 and 15 contain student information that are restricted until January 1, 2077. Restrictions are noted at the file level within the inventory.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Founding Days, correspondence, African American program
Letter to board of directors leadership

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: National African American student leadership summit
AACC Diagnosis by School of Management
Building History [211 Park St], AACC House Proposal
The Tuesday Connection and Minority Staff Statistics Report
Students of Color for Reproductive Health
National Council of Negro Women, Inc. [Yale Chapter]
National Coalition of 100 Black Women, Inc.
Black Graduate Network [BGN] Correspond., Posters, Flyers
Klub Kreyol: Haitian Student Alliance at Yale
Black Arts, Crafts and Book Fair Exhibition
Louis C. Young "Star your Own Black Magazine…"
Resident Group Officers List and Applications
First Annual Black Achievement Awards Nominee
CT Black and Hispanic Visual Artists Alliance
Yale Policy on South Africa-Apartheid Investments
Notes on George W. Crawford, Law 1903
Black Grad and Undergrad Student List
Gloria Naylor, Author, Professor, Feminist Theorist
Correspondence and Grad. Professor Student List
Charles Martin -Letter and Photo of James Baldwin
Correspondence with other AFAM Cultural Centers
Assault on Democracy in the Alabama Black Belt
Report on Free Speech and Disruption
Yale University Affirmative Action Policy [varied years]
Black Tie: News of the Society
Blassingame, John W. essays. "Life and Death of Affirmative Action" and "Old Perspective on Yale's Majority House"
Golden Drums Black Seeds - plus Manuscript
"The Present State of…"- Lecture by Prof. Derrick Bell
Donald Byrd and the Blackbirds in Concert
Southern Africa: The Struggle for Nat19l Liberation
Putney Experience and the Yale Experience
Trees and Circles - R. R. Cohen Exhibit
Extraordinary Evening of Literature and Jazz
"The Women of Brewster Place", Gloria Naylor book signing etc
Nothing But A Man - film screening
"In Her Own Write": Black Women Writers…, U.S. Africa, etc.
"Some Call It Jazz": featuring Menage a Trois
An Evening of Wine and Roses: Winter Cabaret
A Law and Business School Colloquium
Peter Katjavivi on: Namiban Lib Struggle
Special Presentation for Black History Month
"For Colored Girls Who Have Considered Suicide…" [play]
Open House and Orientation for New Students
Initial Jam of the Year Party
The Grenadian Question: Aseklan Heard Perspective
10th Annual Black Community Picnic and Olympics
"On Stripping Bark from Myself," Andi Chapman
Career Fair: Cross-Section of Corp. America
Rhapsody in Crimson and Blue: Yale/Harvard Weekend
Life and Times of M.L.K. [Al Eaton Presents]
Zamani Za Kale: Art of Njee and K. Muturi
Black Arts, Crafts and Book Fair
Dr. M.L.K. Lecture by Naomi Tutu-Seavers
Africa Unadorned: Photo Exhibit by Norma Holt
A Cross Section of Corp. America: A Spectrum of Opportunity
Second Annual Black Arts, Crafts and Book Fair
Down on Rumsey Street [by Denise Byrd]
Jose Buscaglia: Forty Yrs of Sculpture
Black Student Alliance at Yale: "Freedom…" 2 signed copies
"Spell #7" by Ntozake Shange, directed by C. Emery
Call on the One [Yale Gospel Choir]
Angela Y. Davis: Women and the Struggle Against Racism
Feminist Scholars with Degrees from Yale
People of Color Fighting Color in the Persian Gulf
Praise the Lord: Come Join Us for a Time of Worship and Praise
An Evening with "In Progress" - A Concert
A Reception for Cast of "The Colored Museum"
Yale Repretory Theater News: The Colored Museum
The Minority Career Forum, New England
1968-1993: 25, Yale School of Medicine Marks 25thAnniversary of MLK, Jr.
A Night with Campos Parsi: A Recital
A Conversation with Shirley Taylor Haizlip
Mali K.K. Ahmed, Dinner and Lecture
The Continuing Presence of Blacks at Yale
The Spirit of Yale-Harvard: More than a Game
Visions of Africa: Contemporary African Cinema
Photos to be scanned and clippings
Materials relating to the Afro-American Cultural Center at Yale

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until Jan 1, 2040

did.unittitle: Founding of Afro-American/Black Studies - varied material
Four black and white photographs of classmates
Armstead L. Robinson - papers, correspondence, eulogy
Black Alumni Assoc. and Conference [not received]
Oral histories of the Afro-American Cultural Center at Yale

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Permission required from interviewee to cite or quote from material during their lifetime, after which permission must be obtained from the Yale University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: "A proposal for a major in Afro-American Studies…" [photocopy]

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until Jan 1, 2042
